metadata.title: Marhaban Canada | Your arrival in Canada, one click at a time.
metadata.description: Guidance service for newcomers to Canada: official procedures, housing, banking, phone, and fraud prevention.

home.title: Your arrival in Canada, one click at a time.
home.subtitle: Each step is explained simply, with its official sources and no jargon.
home.presentation: Marhaban Canada is a free guidance service that walks you through a successful settlement. No account required, no data collected.
home.whyContent: Because settling in a new country is complicated enough. We simplify official information to save you time and give you peace of mind.

howSteps.title: Consult the checklist
howSteps.description: A clear list of actions to take, sorted by priority.

howSteps.title: Follow the guides
howSteps.description: Each step is detailed: what, why, how, and pitfalls to avoid.

howSteps.title: Go at your own pace
howSteps.description: Check off completed steps. Your progress is saved on your device, and nowhere else.

home.disclaimer: This site is an independent, non-governmental guidance service. Always verify information on official websites.

checklist.title: Settlement Checklist - The Essential Steps
checklist.description: Check off steps as you go. Your progress is saved automatically on your device, no account needed.
checklist.confirmReset: Are you sure you want to reset everything?

week1.subtitle: 🟢 Urgent — to get you up and running quickly.

month1.subtitle: 🟡 Important — to secure your daily life.

nas.title: Get your SIN (Social Insurance Number)
nas.avoid: Never share your SIN via messaging or phone.

phone.title: Get a local phone / SIM plan
phone.avoid: Avoid long contracts at the beginning; prefer prepaid.

bank.title: Open a bank account and get a card
bank.avoid: Always ask for newcomer packages to avoid fees.

health.title: Apply for your provincial health card
health.avoid: Don't wait for an emergency to register; delays can be long.

housing.title: Look for long-term housing
housing.avoid: Never pay a deposit before visiting and signing an official lease.

license.title: Exchange your driver's license (if applicable)
license.avoid: Do not drive without a valid license and local insurance.

integration.title: Integration services and local library
integration.avoid: Avoid staying isolated; settlement services are free.

network.title: Start your professional networking
network.avoid: Don't postpone updating your LinkedIn and making initial contacts.

taxes.title: Understand the basics of the tax system
taxes.avoid: Don't lose your receipts and pay stubs; they are essential.

steps.normalization: Most newcomers complete this step within the first {days} days.

nas.title: Get Your Social Insurance Number (SIN)
nas.summary: The SIN is the key to working legally, paying taxes, and receiving government benefits.
nas.what: It's a unique 9-digit number that Service Canada gives you. It is personal and confidential.
nas.why: Without a SIN, no employer can legally pay you. It is essential for your tax file and access to social programs.
how: Gather your original documents: passport, work or study permit.
Make an appointment at a Service Canada Centre (recommended) or apply online if you are eligible.
Keep the confirmation letter in a safe place. Do not carry your SIN number in your wallet.
avoid: Only give your SIN to your employer (after signing the contract), your bank, and government agencies (taxes).
Refuse any paid offer promising a faster SIN. The service is always free.
Never share a photo of your SIN document via email or messaging.

faq.a: In person, you often get it the same day. Online, it can take several weeks.
Yes, if you plan to work in Canada.

bank.title: Open a Bank Account
bank.summary: Essential for receiving a salary, paying your rent, and starting to build your credit history.
bank.what: A chequing account for daily transactions and a debit card. It's also an opportunity to apply for a secured credit card.
bank.why: A local account simplifies all your financial transactions and proves your solvency for procedures like renting a home.
how: Compare 'newcomer packages' from major banks (RBC, TD, BMO, CIBC, Scotiabank) which often offer 1 year with no fees.
Make an appointment at a branch with your passport, work/study permit, and proof of address (even a temporary one).
Ask for a secured credit card: you deposit a small amount (e.g., $500) which becomes your credit limit. It's the best way to start a good credit history.
avoid: Don't sign up for products (insurance, investments) you don't understand at the first meeting.
Avoid withdrawing money from other banks' ATMs to avoid high fees.
Never share your PIN or online access codes.

faq.q: Can I open an account before arriving?
faq.a: Some banks allow it online, but you will have to finalize it in person upon arrival to verify your identity.

bank.actionButtonLabel: Official Guide to Opening a Bank Account

housing.title: Find Housing
housing.summary: A crucial step that requires preparation to avoid scams and find a safe place.
housing.what: The search for a rental (apartment, condo, house) and the signing of an official lease.
housing.why: A lease protects your rights as a tenant and sets the terms (rent, duration). Stable housing is the foundation of your settlement.
how: Prepare a 'tenant file': letter of employment (or proof of funds), references, copy of your work/study permit.
Use reputable sites (Centris.ca in Quebec, Realtor.ca, Kijiji, Facebook Marketplace with caution).
ALWAYS visit the housing in person or by video call. Verify that the person showing the unit is the owner or their agent.
Read the lease carefully before signing. Pay the security deposit only AFTER signing, and by a traceable method (bank transfer, cheque).
avoid: NEVER transfer money (deposit, first month's rent) before visiting and signing a lease. This is the most common scam.
Refuse requests for a 'deposit to book a viewing'. This is illegal.
Be wary of offers that are too good to be true (very low rent, perfect professional photos).

faq.q: How to do it without a Canadian credit history?
faq.a: Offer to pay several months' rent in advance (if provincial law allows) or provide a guarantor (a Canadian resident).

phone.title: Get a Phone Number
phone.summary: A Canadian phone number is essential for almost all administrative and professional procedures.
phone.what: Buying a SIM card (or eSIM) and choosing a mobile plan (prepaid or monthly).
phone.why: Many services (banks, government) use SMS verification. It is also essential to be contacted by employers.
how: At the airport, buy a prepaid SIM card from an official kiosk (Bell, Rogers, Telus, Freedom) to be connected immediately.
Compare plans from major operators and their low-cost brands (Fido, Koodo, Virgin, Public Mobile).
Opt for a 'Bring Your Own Device' (BYOD) plan if your phone is compatible; it's cheaper and without commitment.
avoid: Avoid 2-year contracts at the beginning. Your situation may change, and you don't have a credit history yet.
Do not buy a SIM card from unofficial street vendors.
Disable data roaming to avoid surprise bills.

health.title: Access Healthcare
health.summary: Registering for your province's health insurance plan is a priority to avoid exorbitant medical costs.
health.what: Getting your provincial health insurance card (RAMQ in Quebec, OHIP in Ontario, etc.).
health.why: It covers essential medical services (doctor, hospital). Without it, a visit to the emergency room can cost thousands of dollars.
how: Check your province of arrival's website for the procedure and required documents (passport, permit, proof of residence).
Apply as soon as you arrive. Some provinces have a waiting period of up to 3 months.
During the waiting period, subscribe to temporary private health insurance. This is crucial.
avoid: Not subscribing to private insurance during the waiting period. An accident can happen at any time.
Lying about your arrival date or address. This can lead to the cancellation of your coverage.
Paying non-governmental sites that offer to fill out forms for you. It's free on official sites.

faq.q: What to do during the 3-month waiting period?
faq.a: You MUST subscribe to private health insurance for newcomers. Many companies offer it.

about.title: About Marhaban Canada
about.mission: To facilitate the settlement of newcomers to Canada by providing clear, reliable, and reassuring information.
about.vision: A Canada where every newcomer can start their new life with confidence and serenity, without getting lost in administrative complexity.
about.howToUse: This site is a guide. It does not replace professional advice. Use the checklist to track your progress, consult the guides to understand each step, and always refer to the official (governmental) sources we systematically link to.
whatWeDontDo: We are not a government service.
We do not provide legal or immigration advice.
We do not fill out any forms for you.
We do not collect or sell any personal data.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.text: For any suggestions or general questions about the site's operation, you can write to us at the following address. Please note that we cannot offer any personalized support for your procedures.
contact.disclaimer: No guarantee of response. No legal or immigration support.

legal.title: Legal Notice & Disclaimer
legal.disclaimer: Marhaban Canada is an independent information site and is not affiliated with the Government of Canada or any other official entity. The information provided is for guidance only and aims to simplify procedures. It in no way replaces the information and procedures available on official government websites. We cannot be held responsible for any errors, omissions, or consequences related to the use of the information on this site.
legal.privacy: We respect your privacy. Marhaban Canada does not collect, store, share, or sell any personal data. Your checklist progress is saved only in your own browser's local storage. If you clear your browser's cache or switch devices, this information will be lost.
